Okay, so this is basically, this is the process, this is the game we're gonna play. We're basically gonna do like a mini power day slash consulting with Russell where we're gonna build out an offer and then we're gonna build out a sales process to match it. Okay, do you wanna be fun? Cool. One of my favorite things, I did my power day with Dustin, these guys, we came in there and he was talking about earlier like how they start with the end first, which was really cool because they came in there, we had my slides down, okay, this is how we're gonna start right here, this is my intro and they're like, no, no, no, no, no, we don't know what your intro. What's selling, what's the thing? And so the very first slide we built out was the slide, this is my offer, like the slide, you've seen a slide that looks like this, this and this is the price point, this is the price point, this is like the whole thing, that was how we built the whole thing, we spent probably an hour just building that and then from there, excuse me, then from there we built out every single slide that kind of went backwards from there, okay. Most people start the opposite way around, okay, everything in this entire business all hinges on the offer. You got a good offer, you're gonna make a lot of money, you got a bad offer, you're not gonna make very much money at all, okay. So it all comes down to what's the offer and if your offer's gonna be sexy, that's more important than anything else, okay. It was interesting when we were at about a month and a half ago, I flew out to Ryan Dice's office and Ryan Dice and Perry Belcher both worked there, so we're sitting down and if you've ever seen there, if you guys have watched their company, what they're doing, really fascinating, they're doing some really cool stuff. They just hired Mary Ellen Tribby, if you guys know anything about her, she's the man, so she takes businesses and blows them up fast. But anyway, their whole company's like this offer lab right now, they come through and they're recruiting people, they find good offers or they're creating some of their own offers and they're testing things. And what's interesting, when I was talking, we were sitting down talking to Ryan, Perry and Mary Ellen, we were at lunch just talking about this whole thing and they all just, they kept, I'm sorry, how much it's just like this story, like how good is this story? If you've got a good story, everything else will flow from there. If you've got the right offer, the right story, everything else just flows from there, like you can build a whole campaign around it, it's interesting. We do a lot of work with a bunch of the call centers down in Utah, one of the biggest call center in Utah is called PMI, a huge building down there. They're also partners with Dean Grasio, they do all of Dean's back end, Dean does all the infomercials that brings the leads in. And Dean does a whole bunch of different people's infomercials, I was sharing this upstairs with dinner with some of you guys tonight. But Dean did Anthony Morrison's infomercial and a bunch of other ones. Okay, do you guys see Anthony Morrison's infomercial? You guys haven't seen this, you guys have? That thing is making crazy money, like crazy, crazy money. They're doing like between four and five thousand leads a week off of that thing, making a lot of money in the front, but then crazy money on the back. So the whole thing though, when they came to Anthony, all they wanted from, like Anthony is just like any of us sitting out in the audience. He's just a kid, but he had a good story and a good hook, good offer, and they built a whole campaign around it. Now he's making millions of dollars just based off the story alone. Companies that are like PMI and they come in there, they don't care about how good you are, don't care about anything else. All they care about is you've got a good hook and a good story. For example, they actually, they did an offer with a guy and the whole concept of the offer was like, you buy something on Craigslist for this price, sell it on eBay for this price. And they found this guy and that was the whole thing. But so the concept was good, but the story wasn't that good. They actually created infomercial, they rolled it out and it didn't do very good at all. So they went back out and they just started searching around trying to find someone else in the world that was doing that and they found this guy, kind of a bigger overweight guy. They had no name, no list or anything, but he had a little info product teaching how he did that process. So they came back, they said, hey, we want to test out, you've got the content, you've got a story, you've got a really good story. They created infomercial around, tested the offer, it converted great, now a guy's making crazy money. All he did was just leverage his story. Okay? So I had a call from a young man who's just been coming to my website and the blog and reading what I've been doing and at four at 14 years old he was told that his bladder condition was so hopeless that there was nothing that they could do for him. So he's been suffering with this since he was five six years old. They diagnosed him with overactive bladder. The urologist finally sent him to his nurse who specialized in counseling people that have overactive bladder how to be able to deal with it. What her advice to him was were the types of incontinent pads and diapers for him to wear at 14. And so he came to my website last year. He called me up on the phone. I've talked with him several times. He was having to go to the bathroom like constantly and he was pee in the bed all night long and everything. So just with a little bit of coaching that I've done with him over the phone. He now is pretty much got it all under control. He has an accident maybe once a month now and his time between toileting is back to normal. Have you ever been on the news? No. Who has been on news before? Okay. This is like, it's easy. It's not that expensive. It's like the best street cred you can get is being on the news. Like it's the easiest thing. Like when we used to, we hired a PR company, paying a couple thousand bucks and they made a couple phone calls and boom, we were on the news like the next day. Like, I would, that's something that everyone should invest in. And plan invest in two or three thousand dollars. With it, at least the PR agents that we hired, they wanted to tie it to some kind of event. So they would say, okay Russell, next week let's have you fly to Salt Lake City, Utah. And you're going to do a one hour event in a hotel room. Like, they didn't care what it was. They just wanted an event. And they would go and they'd spend like a week and a half calling every single media outlet in Salt Lake saying, Russell Brunson's coming to do a live event, live event, live event, live event. They'd call all the radio, all the TV, all the newspaper, all these different things. And we'd get one or two people to say yes, they'll cover it. I come down and do this a little quick, like one hour event. But the good thing is I'm on the news doing like three or four of these little clips.
